front: Poem: opens 'You do not do, you do not do / Any more, black shoe'; the father is 'a man in black with a Meinkampf look' and 'Not God but a swastika'
Poem: the speaker claims 'Every woman adores a Fascist' and 'I think I may be a bit of a Jew'; ends 'There's a stake in your fat black heart... You bastard, I'm through'
Poem: 'the snows of the Tyrol, the clear beer of Vienna are not very pure or true'; the speaker says 'I thought every German was you'
Poem: the father's cleft chin is identified in a photograph at the blackboard; the speaker claims 'If I've killed one man, I've killed two'
Poem: declares 'Dying / Is an art, like everything else. I do it exceptionally well'; addresses 'Herr Doktor' and 'Herr Lucifer' with 'Beware, beware'
Poem: the speaker states 'I am your opus, I am your valuable' and 'there is a charge for the hearing of my heart'; says 'One year in every ten I manage it'
Poem: opens with 'Stasis in darkness'; the speaker is 'the arrow, the dew that flies suicidal' and flies 'Into the red / Eye, the cauldron of morning'
Poem: 'White Godiva, I unpeel- / Dead hands, dead stringencies'; 'the child's cry melts in the wall'
Poem: opens 'I shall never get you put together entirely'; the father is 'pithy and historical as the Roman Forum'
Poem: the speaker tells the addressee 'You defy questions; You defy godhood' and concludes 'this thick air is murderous. I would breathe water'
Poem: inspired by a Giorgio de Chirico painting; ends 'no frown of mine will betray the company I keep'; asks mother about the titular figures
Novel: the protagonist sees her life branching 'like a fig tree'; she becomes ill from crab meat at the Amazon hotel after meeting boss Jay Cee
Novel: the protagonist tries to write a thesis on twin imagery in Finnegans Wake while sponsored by Philomena Guinea; masquerades as 'Elly Higginbottom'
Novel: the protagonist receives electroconvulsive therapy; Dr. Nolan advises her to get a diaphragm; boyfriend Buddy Willard called a hypocrite after watching Mrs. Tomolillo give birth
Poem: the title objects 'eat my oxygen' and open 'like the mouth of some great African cat'; written during recovery from an appendectomy
Poem: opens 'Love set you going like a fat gold watch'; the speaker rises 'cow-heavy and floral'; baby's 'clear vowels rise like balloons'
Poem: opens 'Perfection is terrible, it cannot have children'; a city is called a 'morgue between Paris and Rome' with phones 'Glittering and digesting Voicelessness'
Author: Heather Clark's Red Comet (2020) and Janet Malcolm's The Silent Woman discuss biographical disputes; Al Alvarez wrote The Savage God
Author: Robin Morgan's poem 'Arraignment' blames this poet's death on her ex-husband, who had an affair with Assia Wevill
Poem: 'Little pilgrim, the Indian's axed your scalp'; about accidentally cutting a thumb instead of an onion
Poem: villanelle that repeats 'I think I made you up inside my head' and 'I fancied you'd return the way you said'
